title: Kaggle launches a multi-step tool-attack competition for AI agent security
friendlyTitle: Kaggle opens a competition to stress-test AI agents
summary: Kaggle’s AI Agent Security: Multi-Step Tool Attacks competition asks participants to build attack algorithms that find reproducible failures in tool-using agents. The benchmark focuses on multi-step paths from untrusted inputs to unsafe actions, including exfiltration, destructive writes, and confused-deputy behavior.
friendlySummary: The competition challenges participants to find reproducible multi-step failures in tool-using agents, such as leaking data or taking unsafe actions after reading untrusted content.
whyItMatters: Agent security needs benchmarks that test actions, not just harmful text. A replay-validated competition gives researchers and builders a shared way to study prompt injection, tool misuse, and real-world failure chains before those failures reach production systems.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This helps the AI community test agent safety in a more realistic way. Agents that use tools need security checks for what they do, not only what they say.
